Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington.description: An outlaw motorcycle club founded in Bloomington, California in 1945 by Norman Briggs. The club participated in the 1947 Hollister riot, which inspired the film The Wild One. It is considered a precursor to the Hells Angels, as a former member founded that club. The club remains active in California, Nevada, and Utah.

descriptions: Both clubs participated together in the 1947 Hollister incident.
edges.evidence_quote: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington , participated in the highly publicized Hollister incident

descriptions: POBOB and Boozefighters jointly participated in the 1947 Hollister riot.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington (POBOB) is a motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Market Street Commandos , participated in the highly publicized Hollister riot

descriptions: Hells Angels was founded by a former POBOB member who broke away from POBOB over a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: One former Pissed Off Bastards member, Otto Friedli, is credited with founding the Hells Angels in Fontana after breaking from the POBOB over a feud with a rival gang.

descriptions: POBOB and Market Street Commandos jointly participated in the 1947 Hollister riot.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington (POBOB) is a motorcycle club that, in 1947, along with the Boozefighters and the Market Street Commandos , participated in the highly publicized Hollister riot

edges.target: Holy Land Foundation for Relief and Development
descriptions: Holy Land Foundation channeled money to Hamas.
edges.evidence_quote: The US, Israel and the EU have shut down charities and organs that channel money to Hamas, such as the Holy Land Foundation for Relief and Development.

descriptions: The Norman Conks had friendly relations with the Calton Tongs, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: but were on friendlier terms with the Calton Tongs who were also mostly Catholic

Tongan Crip Gang.description: The Tongan Crip Gang is a Tongan American street gang and a subset of the Crips alliance, founded in Inglewood, California. It is active in California, Utah, Alaska, Washington, and parts of Auckland, New Zealand. The gang engages in a wide range of criminal activities including drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. It was involved in a 2007 federal indictment for a conspiracy to murder members of MS-13.

descriptions: Tongan Crip Gang and 18th Street Gang conspired together to murder MS-13 members.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2007, members of the Tongan Crip Gang and the 18 Street Gang were indicted by a federal grand jury for criminal conspiracy in a plot to murder 33 members of a Los Angeles subset of MS-13.

descriptions: Baby Regulators is listed as a rival of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kearns Town Bloods, Baby Regulators, Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips, Carver Park Crips

descriptions: Tongan Crip Gang is a subgroup of the Crips alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tongan Crip Gang ( TCG ) is a Tongan American street gang that is a subgroup, or " set ", of the Crips gang alliance.

descriptions: Kearns Town Bloods is listed as a rival of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kearns Town Bloods, Baby Regulators, Lennox 13 Park Village Compton Crips, Carver Park Crips

edges.evidence_quote: Giovanni Brusca from San Giuseppe Jato

10th & Oregon Crew.description: The 10th & Oregon Crew is a predominantly Italian American organized crime gang operating in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, and South Jersey. Founded in the 1960s by Rocco Turra, the gang is active in drug trafficking, gambling, extortion, and loan sharking. It has both allied with and feuded against the Philadelphia crime family. The gang remains active as of the present.

descriptions: The gang had multiple run-ins with rival street gang 12th & Wharton in the early to late 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: the 10th & Oregon Crew had multiple run-ins with a rival street gang, 12th & Wharton

descriptions: The gang rivaled the Pagans for drug dealing and loansharking territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The 10th & Oregon Crew rivaled the Pagans for drug dealing and loansharking territory.

descriptions: Infobox lists Pagan's MC as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Pagan's MC

descriptions: The gang has been both allied with and feuded against the Philadelphia crime family at various times.
edges.evidence_quote: Under different leadership, the group has both been allied closely with and violently feuded against the Philadelphia crime family at various times.

descriptions: The gang has been both allied with and feuded against the Philadelphia crime family at various times.
edges.evidence_quote: Under different leadership, the group has both been allied closely with and violently feuded against the Philadelphia crime family at various times.

descriptions: Infobox lists Philadelphia crime family as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: The gang is associated with but independent from the Philadelphia crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang is associated with but independent from the Philadelphia crime family

descriptions: The American Mafia is allied with the 10th & Oregon Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sicilian Mafia, Camorra, 'Ndrangheta, various independent Italian-American gangs (such as the 10th & Oregon Crew, Purple Gang, and Tanglewood Boys)

descriptions: The American Mafia lists 10th & Oregon Crew as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Sicilian Mafia , Camorra , 'Ndrangheta , Sacra corona unita , varias pandillas independientes ítaloestadounidenses (como la 10th & Oregon Crew , Purple Gang , South Brooklyn Boys , y Tanglewood Boys )

descriptions: Merlino's men beat Turra for failing to pay street tax, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Louis Turra, leader of a Philadelphia drug gang, the 10th & Oregon Crew, was severely beaten by Merlino's men, allegedly for failing to pay a Mafia street tax on the gang's illegal earnings.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 10th & Oregon Crew

'Ndrina Crea.description: The 'Ndrina Crea is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Rizziconi, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in organized crime activities such as drug trafficking and extortion. The clan has been led by various bosses including Teodoro Crea and Giuseppe Crea, and has been targeted in multiple police operations. As of recent reports, the clan remains active.

descriptions: Ascone and Crea were in conflict in the Rizziconi feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Rizziconi , esplosa tra gli Ascone e i Crea.

descriptions: The Crea 'ndrina helped Umberto Bellocco reassert leadership.
edges.evidence_quote: Umberto Bellocco viene scarcerato il 14 aprile 2014... avrebbe tentato di riaffermare la propria leadership, con l'aiuto dei Crea

descriptions: Novella mediated peace between Gallace and Crea di Siderno.
edges.evidence_quote: Carmelo Novella fece da paciere tra i Gallace e i Crea di Siderno invocando l'aiuto di Antonio Pelle.

descriptions: Lo Presti's successor Luciano Ursino entered business relations with the Crea brothers, bosses of the Turin 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Luciano Ursino, entra in rapporti di affari con i fratelli Adolfo e Aldo Cosimo Crea, originari di Stilo , in Calabria , boss emergenti della 'Ndrangheta torinese.

descriptions: Bahala Na Gang was formed to counter the Sige-Sige Gang, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: To counter the existing Sige-Sige Gang, Divino formed the Bahala Na Gang

descriptions: Satan Slaves MC rejected El Forastero's application for a chapter, leading to El Forastero's independent founding.
edges.evidence_quote: El Forastero Motorcycle Club ( EFMC ) is a one-percenter motorcycle club which was established after being turned down for a chapter by the Satan Slaves MC.

Bugs and Meyer Mob.description: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was a Jewish-American street gang based in Manhattan's Lower East Side, formed and headed by Bugsy Siegel and Meyer Lansky during their teenage years shortly after Prohibition. The gang engaged in murder, gambling, bootlegging, theft, racketeering, and extortion, and acted as a predecessor to Murder, Inc. Lansky was considered the brains, while Siegel was the brawn. The mob was active from the 1920s to the 1930s.

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob helped organize the modern American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: During the Castellammarese War, Lansky and Siegel helped Luciano eliminate the "Mustache Petes" and organize the modern American Mafia.

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob worked with Broadway Mob in the 1920s.
edges.evidence_quote: Lansky and Siegel, being longtime associates of Luciano, would frequently employ the gang to work with Joe Adonis's Broadway Mob throughout the 1920s.

descriptions: Bugs and Meyer Mob was a predecessor to Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bugs and Meyer mob acted as a predecessor to Murder, Inc.

descriptions: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was absorbed into Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: the Jewish Bugs and Meyer Mob operated on the Lower East Side of Manhattan before being absorbed into Murder, Inc.

descriptions: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was the predecessor to Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bugs and Meyer Mob was the predecessor to Murder, Incorporated.

descriptions: The Mokotów group splintered from the Pruszków mafia in 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: In the same year, a group originating from the Pruszków mafia, known as the Mokotów group, splintered off and continued to act independently until 2014.

descriptions: Maggapa was a copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family.
edges.evidence_quote: At the same time as the 1996 North Korean commandos infiltration incident , there was a South Korean nine-member copycat gang inspired by the Chijon Family. The copycat gang called themselves the Maggapa (막가파).

Mickey Cobras.description: The Mickey Cobras are an African American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, founded in 1954 as the Egyptian Cobras by James Cogwell. The gang is part of the People Nation alliance and is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. It was renamed in honor of Henry "Mickey" Cogwell after his assassination in 1977. The gang remains active in Chicago and the Midwestern United States.

descriptions: Mickey Cobras are a splinter group that split from BPSN.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mickey Cobras were supporters of Mickey Cogwell, a co-founder of BPSN killed by Jeff Fort.

descriptions: The Vice Lords list Mickey Cobras as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black P. Stones , Bloods , Latin Kings , People Nation , Mickey Cobras

descriptions: Infobox lists Mickey Cobras as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples ( conflict ) , Vice Lords , Black P. Stones , Mickey Cobras

descriptions: Egyptian Cobras fought with the 14th Street Clovers in the mid-1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: During the mid-1950s the Egyptian Cobras fought heavily with a gang called the 14th Street Clovers.

descriptions: The Cobras (then Cobras Stones) allied with Black P. Stone Nation against common enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cobras and the Black P. Stone Nation allied against the Disciple Alliance and later the Gangster Nation.

descriptions: The Vice Lords became dominant over the Egyptian Cobras after returning to Lawndale.
edges.evidence_quote: When the Vice Lords returned to Lawndale in the year 1958, they soon swelled into a force to be reckoned with. The Vice Lords were very aggressive and the dominant gang in Lawndale.

the commander of the Gang Intelligence Unit for the Chicago Police Department portrayed Cogwell as the link between gangs and organized crime , particularly the Chicago Outfit .

edges.evidence_quote: Among initial members to the People were the Mickey Cobras (then named the Cobra Stones), Bishops, Spanish Lords, soon after the Gaylords and the Insane South Side Popes.

edges.evidence_quote: a major disturbance erupted in the Gorbals as the Beehive Boys and the South Side Stickers reportedly joined forces to confront police officers

descriptions: MNDAA succeeded CPB control in Kokang after CPB disbanded.
edges.evidence_quote: From the 1960s to 1989, the Kokang area in northern Shan state was controlled by the Communist Party of Burma, and after the party's armed wing disbanded in 1989 it became a special region of Myanmar under the control of the Myanmar Nationalities Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)

descriptions: NDAA is a splinter group that broke away from CPB.
edges.evidence_quote: A splinter group led by Sai Leun which broke away from the CPB at the same time established the National Democratic Alliance Army (NDAA)

descriptions: MNDAA attacked TNLA, breaking their alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In mid-March 2026, the alliance between MNDAA and TNLA broke down over the issue of security cameras and to reopen the trade route to China. The MNDAA attacked TNLA-controlled Kutkai using drones and took the city on 16 March.

descriptions: MNDAA and TNLA are both members of the Northern Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: the Northern Alliance—which consists of four insurgent groups, the Arakan Army (AA), the Kachin Independence Army (KIA), the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA) and the Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA)

descriptions: After conflict, MNDAA and TNLA pledged friendship and cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On the 17th anniversary of the founding of the Arakan Army, the MNDAA and TNLA pledged to continue their friendship, as well as cooperate with the AA and to work together until the 'revolutionary vision' is achieved.

descriptions: MNDAA and TNLA conduct joint attacks against the Tatmadaw.
edges.evidence_quote: The MNDAA and Ta'ang National Liberation Army (TNLA) have carried out several joint attacks against the Tatmadaw in Shan State.

descriptions: UWSA has close links with MNDAA.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA maintains close links with FPNCC partners like the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)

descriptions: UWSA is allied with NDAA.
edges.evidence_quote: it serves as a link with its ally, the National Democratic Alliance Army (NDAA) in Mongla

Rocks Push.description: A notorious larrikin gang that dominated The Rocks area of Sydney from the 1870s to the 1890s. Engaged in theft, murder, assault, and robbery, often using female members to lure victims. Leaders were determined through bare-knuckle boxing matches. Known for a distinctive dress style to appear unarmed while concealing razor blades.

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Argyle Cut Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Forty Thieves.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Gibb Street Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Glebe Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Larry Foley, leader of the Greens, later became a leader of the Rocks Push after defeating Sandy Ross.
edges.evidence_quote: Larry Foley , later to be regarded as the 'Father of Australian Boxing', was the leader of a Roman Catholic larrikin gang known as the Greens.

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Miller's Point Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

Nacionalni stroj.description: Nacionalni stroj was a neo-Nazi organization based in the Vojvodina region of Serbia. It orchestrated several incidents from 2005 onward. In late 2005, charges were pressed against 18 leading members in Novi Sad, each facing up to eight years in prison. The organization was banned in Serbia in 2011.

Rockers Motor Club.description: Canadian outlaw biker gang and support club for the Hells Angels, founded in Montreal on March 26, 1992 by Maurice 'Mom' Boucher. Active until 2001, it served as a recruitment tool and enforcement arm for the Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War, carrying out intimidation, assaults, and assassinations to control the illegal drug trade.

descriptions: Rockers MC member Gregory Woolley became an associate of Hells Angels Montreal charter.
edges.evidence_quote: Woolley became an associate of the Hells Angels Montreal charter in the 1990s

descriptions: Rockers Motor Club is a puppet club of Hells Angels in Montreal.
edges.evidence_quote: Every Quebec region had its own puppet club: the Rockers Motor Club in Montreal, the Rowdy Ones in Sorel, the Evil Ones in Drummondville, the Satan's Guard in the Saguenay region, and the Jokers in St-Jean

descriptions: The Hells Angels controlled the Rockers as a puppet club.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, Stadnick had the Angels' puppet club in Montreal, the Rockers, set up a puppet club in Winnipeg called the Redliners.

descriptions: Rockers are a Hells Angels Montreal puppet gang.
edges.evidence_quote: two members of the Hells Angels Montreal puppet gang, the Rockers

descriptions: Rockers Motor Club is a puppet club of the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hells Angels created a puppet club , the Rockers Motor Club

descriptions: The Rockers were a puppet club of the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Rockers are a puppet gang of the Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: It was created to counter Hell's Angels allies, the Rockers MC, Evil ones and Death Riders MC support clubs.

edges.evidence_quote: Found inside of the car that Lamer and Bouffard were using were ... pictures of every single Bandido in Ontario.

edges.evidence_quote: a longstanding HAMC-affiliated outlaw motorcycle club based in Laval, Quebec known as the Death Riders MC assimilated into a new chapter of the Rockers Motor Club which became known as Rockers MC North.

descriptions: Evil Ones MC is listed as an ally of Rockers MC in the infobox.

descriptions: Palmers MC is listed as a rival of Rockers MC in the infobox.

descriptions: The Rockers established the Redliners as a puppet club.

descriptions: Rockers MC founded the Redliners as an auxiliary club.
edges.evidence_quote: Walter "Nurget" Stadnick instructed the Rockers Motor Club to found an auxiliary outlaw motorcycle club of their own which would be based out of out west in Winnipeg, Manitoba . The resulting one-percenter supporter club that came about was the Redliners

descriptions: Rockers MC was involved in the war against Rock Machine, carrying out attacks.

descriptions: Hamas attacked and fought Jund Ansar Allah in a battle.

descriptions: The Charles Birger gang and Shelton Brothers Gang fought against the Ku Klux Klan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang and rival Charles Birger gang engaged in a war with the Ku Klux Klan that concluded with a shoot out at Herrin.

descriptions: The Shelton Brothers Gang and Charles Birger's gang were at war.
edges.evidence_quote: The Shelton Brothers Gang engaged in a war with Charles Birger 's gang that concluded, in 1925, when the Shelton Brothers were convicted of the murder of a mail carrier.
